name: Baked Peameal Bacon
recipeInstructions:
Peameal bacon is a unique Canadian meat, pickled but unsmoked pork loin rolled in cornmeal. Usually it is sliced and fried for breakfast, but it excels when baked whole. The cornmeal creates a crisp exterior, and the lean meat remains juicy due to the pickling process. For baking, select a piece from the center cut with a wide band of lean visible on both ends.
1. Place the peameal bacon in an open roasting pan, fat side up.
2. Bake at 350°F for 1-1/2 hours.
3. Serve hot in generous slices; pair with baked beans if desired.
